items.headline: Mountain View updates biodiversity and urban forest plan
items.summary: The City Council held a study session to review a draft Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan on April 28, signaling potential changes to how the city manages trees, green spaces, and wildlife habitat. This plan could affect tree preservation rules, landscaping requirements, and parks development across Mountain View.

items.headline: Foothills Nature Preserve improvements project moves forward
items.summary: Council is receiving an informational update on the Foothills Nature Preserve Improvements Project (PE-21000), indicating planned physical enhancements to this public natural area.

items.headline: Middle school athletics program management model under review
items.summary: Council will discuss the future management and direction of Palo Alto's middle school athletics program, suggesting potential changes to how the program is run or funded.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed: 67 homes plus retail in Sunnyvale
items.summary: A 4.22-acre development project would add 55 townhomes and 12 apartments alongside 18,535 square feet of retail space, including a new two-story building while preserving Speedy's Tacos. The Planning Commission held a study session on April 27 to review the special development permit and subdivision map.

items.headline: Protected tree removal disputed at 751 Waverley Street
items.summary: A previously approved tree removal permit at 751 Waverley Street is being reviewed again after a director's appeal. This suggests neighborhood concern about the loss of protected trees on the property.

items.headline: Campbell updates business license tax rates
items.summary: The City Council considered an ordinance to revise business license tax rates under the Campbell Municipal Code as part of the annual fiscal update. The change affects local business licensing costs, though specific rate details are not provided in the agenda summary.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Santa Clara approves massive stadium signage for tournament
items.summary: The City Council authorized temporary supergraphic signs on building surfaces around Levi's Stadium to promote FIFA World Cup 2026 events. This represents a notable change to the city's visual landscape during the tournament period.

items.headline: Milpitas updates development fees and user charges for FY 2026-27
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution establishing new development fees and various user charges for the upcoming fiscal year. These fee changes affect costs for residents and businesses applying for permits and city services.
